title: Scope A T2: add runExclusive<T>(path, fn) to shared atomic-write LedgerWriteQueue
description: Expose a per-path exclusive-execution primitive `runExclusive<T>(path, fn): Promise<T>` that reuses the existing `LedgerWriteQueue` per-path Promise chain so rotation read-partition-write sequences are serialized against ledger appends within a single Node process.

files.change: Add method `runExclusive<T>(path: string, fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T>` to the existing `LedgerWriteQueue` (or as a free function tapping the same per-path Promise map). Returns the value resolved by `fn`. Errors thrown by `fn` reject the returned Promise without breaking the chain (next runExclusive can still acquire). Export as named export.

Add unit tests: (1) sequential ordering — two concurrent runExclusive calls on same path execute in submission order; (2) cross-path parallelism — concurrent calls on different paths run in parallel; (3) error in fn doesn't deadlock subsequent calls; (4) interleave with LedgerWriteQueue.append() — append after runExclusive waits for the exclusive section.

implementation: Read existing LedgerWriteQueue impl to understand per-path Promise-chain pattern
Add `runExclusive<T>` either as a method on LedgerWriteQueue (preferred) or as a sibling export that reuses the same per-path map
Ensure error propagation: rejection from `fn` resolves the chain slot (so next caller proceeds) but rethrows to the original caller
Add 4 unit tests covering ordering, cross-path parallelism, error isolation, and interleave with append

reference.pattern: Existing per-path Promise chain in LedgerWriteQueue
reference.examples: LedgerWriteQueue.append uses `this.queues.get(path) ?? Promise.resolve()` chaining — runExclusive should hook into the same map

criteria: `runExclusive` exported from atomic-write module and referenced from rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded in T3
Unit test suite covers 4 scenarios listed above, all green
Concurrent runExclusive + append on same path serialize correctly (verified by ordering assertion in tests)
Error in `fn` does NOT block subsequent runExclusive calls on the same path (verified by test 3)
Type signature: `runExclusive<T>(path: string, fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T>` — `T` properly inferred

rationale.chosen_approach: Reuse existing per-path Promise chain — zero new abstraction surface
alternatives_considered: proper-lockfile (cross-process — out of rc.22 scope, TODO documented in memory)
Mutex library (extra dep, no value over Promise chain)
decision_factors: Same-process serialization sufficient for current rotation flow
rationale.tradeoffs: Doesn't solve MCP server + standalone doctor cross-process race (already acknowledged gap)

manual_checks: Inspect that runExclusive method doc-comment describes exclusivity scope (per-path, in-process)
